The efficiency claim that gas furnaces 'can never exceed 100%' is slightly misleading—condensing furnaces reach ~98% AFUE, but the core point is valid. Voice is warm and reader-aware ('your refrigerator,' 'you pay for') though it stays safely informational without much personality. Overall an excellent piece that hits the brief squarely."}

rationale: Exceptionally clear and well-organized explainer. The refrigerator analogy grounds the concept immediately, and the numbered benefits flow logically. Language is plain and direct throughout—no jargon or filler. Specificity is strong (300-400% efficiency figure, carbon monoxide mention) though it could note cold-climate performance limits or cost considerations for fuller precision. The efficiency claim that gas furnaces 'can never exceed 100%' is slightly misleading—condensing furnaces reach ~98% AFUE, but the core point is valid. Voice is warm and reader-aware ('your refrigerator,' 'you pay for') though it stays safely informational without much personality. Overall an excellent piece that hits the brief squarely.
